73662.subject: What is your maximum heart rate?
73662.body:
The Economist’s week ahead

Автор: The Economist


Автор: The Economist

    
    
    Прочитайте цю статтю на LinkedIn, щоб приєднатися до обговорення
Прочитати на LinkedIn
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/what-your-maximum-heart-rate-the-economist-fwxne?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_02%3Bcvo7kR52Rr24hgZenUdeOA%3D%3D&midToken=AQFTjFOONAfT2w&midSig=1i-2REoslBFsg1&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_-null-fz96fz~mpnwgo1p~su-null-null&eid=fz96fz-mpnwgo1p-su
  
Sign up for free here to receive this newsletter in your inbox every Saturday. You’ll also get our weekly newsletter with an exclusive introduction from our editor-in-chief. For someone who spends their days thinking about science and data, I’m oddly low-tech when it comes to exercise. I have one of the most basic sports watches on the market and I usually leave the built-in heart-rate monitor turned off. Whenever I train for anything—in the past few years I’ve been into trail marathons—I plan my workouts based on feeling. I just find it more enjoyable that way. But for runners who prefer a more structured approach, one of the first steps is often to figure out their maximum heart rate. Many training plans are built around heart-rate “zones”, which are defined as either percentages of maximum heart rate or as fractions of the range between resting and maximum. If you’re getting into running—or simply curious about the commonest method of calculating your maximum heart rate—read this edition of Well Informed. Our column on health and well-being also has a newsletter you can sign up for. It lands in your inbox every Sunday. Ainslie Johnstone, Data journalist and science correspondent Editor’s picks What is your maximum heart rate? We tell you how to find out Can high-intensity interval training get you fit in a hurry? Yes, but be prepared to suffer What is the best way to train for a marathon?  Most people train too fast The most useful indicator of your overall health “Heart-rate variability” has been decades in the making Are barefoot shoes good for runners? Aficionados swear by them. But the scientific jury is out Go beyond the headlines Get unlimited digital access to The Economist by subscribing today.
Приєднатися до обговорення
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/what-your-maximum-heart-rate-the-economist-fwxne?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_02%3Bcvo7kR52Rr24hgZenUdeOA%3D%3D&midToken=AQFTjFOONAfT2w&midSig=1i-2REoslBFsg1&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-newsletter_content_preview_text-0-readmore_button_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-newsletter_content_preview_text-0-readmore_button_-null-fz96fz~mpnwgo1p~su-null-null&eid=fz96fz-mpnwgo1p-su

73661.subject: Claude isn't done surprising us
73661.body:
----------
######                      [Sign Up](http://utm.genai.works/r/gg2ubr5) | [Collaborate with us](https://6hhfxazcbpc.typeform.com/to/uXIl0IFH?utm_source=atlas&utm_medium=newsletter&utm_campaign=general_2026_05_11&utm_content=banner_post)


--------------------
View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/8776fa6e-e2d0-4aec-9648-1bed6de3a659/GenAI_Daily_Banner.jpg?t=1778687445)
Caption: 


----------

----------
**Welcome back!** Anthropic gave Claude Code a security reviewer that reads its own work, Sam Altman walked back his warning about an AI jobs apocalypse and Spotify defended putting AI music in front of paying listeners. Bold bets across the board.

———————————————————————————

**In today's Generative AI Newsletter:**

* **Claude Code:** What happens when Claude starts reviewing the code it just wrote?

* **Sam Altman:** Why is the man who warned about an AI jobs apocalypse now saying he got it wrong?

* **Spotify:** Is "better than slop" a good enough defense for AI-generated music?

* **Claude Workflows:** What can Claude Code do once you script it to run on its own?


----------

----------
## [Claude learned to check its own code for vulnerabilities](https://x.com/ClaudeDevs/status/2059385239781384341?s=20)

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/3535a267-4cf3-47cc-bcff-99bb2a47ebe7/ChatGPT_Image_May_27__2026__12_26_15_PM.png?t=1779874139)
Caption: 

Anthropic shipped a security-guidance plugin for Claude Code that reviews the code Claude writes and fixes the problems it finds in the same session. 

It’s free for every Claude Code user and installs from the plugin marketplace.

**The details:**

* **Three layers:** A fast pattern match runs on file edits, a background review reads the full diff at the end of each turn and a deeper agentic check reads the surrounding code on every commit or push Claude makes.

* **Independence:** The model that wrote the code is not the one grading it. Uses a separate Claude call with a different prompt.

* **Custom rules:** Teams can add their own checks in a [claude-security-guidance.md](https://claude-security-guidance.md) file or a security-patterns.yaml, and administrators can push org-wide rules through device management.

* **Benchmarks:** Across Anthropic's internal rollout and testing, PRs opened with the plugin drew 30 to 40% fewer security comments.

Building this on hooks means any team can see exactly how it works and attach their own checks at the same points in Claude's loop. 

A 30 to 40% drop in security comments is the kind of number that gets a plugin mandated across a company. If it holds up outside Anthropic's own benchmarks, the pull request stops being the first place anyone hunts for vulnerabilities.


----------

----------

73633.body:
AI-Curated Daily Crypto Digest

Автор: Oleh Bezuhlyi


Автор: Oleh Bezuhlyi

    
    
    Прочитайте цю статтю на LinkedIn, щоб приєднатися до обговорення
Прочитати на LinkedIn
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/ai-curated-daily-crypto-digest-may-26-oleh-bezuhlyi-6oz2f?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_02%3BdaxsjY1hSDecvCtiCgkJug%3D%3D&midToken=AQFTjFOONAfT2w&midSig=0QmXSNV2ECEsg1&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_-null-fz96fz~mpmtulqi~gs-null-null&eid=fz96fz-mpmtulqi-gs
  
🧠 AI’s Crypto News Brief: Markets enter cautious consolidation as Bitcoin volatility hits historic lows. While institutional accumulation from firms like Strive persists, heavy ETF outflows signal cooling retail demand. Regulatory clarity via the CLARITY Act remains the primary catalyst. 📉Bitcoin Volatility Hits 9-Month Low Amid $77K Consolidation Bitcoin’s expected volatility has dropped to its lowest level since 2023 as the asset struggles to break the $80,000 resistance. Market analysts point to $1 billion in monthly ETF outflows as a sign of temporary cooling in retail demand. Read full story 💰Strive Asset Management Adds 1,109 Bitcoin to Treasury Strive (ASST) has increased its total holdings to 16,500 BTC, positioning itself as the seventh-largest public corporate holder globally. The firm continues a strategy of aggressive accumulation to provide shareholders with direct digital asset exposure. See details 🏛️Digital Asset Market Clarity Act Advances Through Senate The CLARITY Act has successfully passed the Senate Banking Committee, moving closer to a final July 4 deadline. The legislation aims to unify US crypto oversight and provide the regulatory certainty needed for multi-trillion dollar institutional entries. Read full story 🚨White House Executive Order Mandates Regulatory Reassessment President Trump signed a directive ordering federal agencies to eliminate outdated barriers hindering digital asset integration. The order specifically targets the removal of restrictive accounting rules and encourages bank-grade crypto custody. See details 💎Bitmine Immersion Holdings Reach 4.4% of Total Ether Supply Bitmine (BMNR) now holds 5.39 million ETH, valued at over $10 billion, following a massive staking-focused accumulation drive. The firm's total crypto and cash reserves have reached a milestone of $12.3 billion. Read full story 🚀NEAR Protocol Surges 15% on AI-Native Blockchain Upgrades Defying a broader market dip, NEAR Protocol hit a six-month high driven by anticipation of the v2.13 network upgrade. Investors are increasingly positioning in NEAR due to its specialized architecture for high-performance AI operations. See details 📊Crypto Funds Record $1.47 Billion in Weekly Outflows Global digital asset funds experienced their third-largest outflow of 2026 as investors pulled capital from major Bitcoin products. However,
